problems.title: Total Sales
problems.description: Calculate the total sales for all products using the SUM function.
problems.hint: Use =SUM( range ) where range is B2:B5
problems.explanation: SUM adds all numbers in a range. =SUM(B2:B5) adds 100+250+175+300 = 825.

problems.title: Total Expenses
problems.description: Calculate the total expenses across all categories using the SUM function.

problems.title: Average Score
problems.description: Find the average test score for all students using the AVERAGE function.
problems.explanation: AVERAGE computes the arithmetic mean. =AVERAGE(B2:B6) = (85+92+78+95+88)/5 = 87.6.

problems.title: Average Temperature
problems.description: Calculate the average daily temperature using the AVERAGE function.
problems.hint: Use =AVERAGE(B2:B7) and round to 2 decimal places

problems.title: Count Orders
problems.description: Count how many orders are in the list using the COUNT function.
problems.hint: Use =COUNT( range ) to count numeric values
problems.explanation: COUNT counts cells containing numbers. =COUNT(A2:A6) = 5.

problems.title: Count Employees
problems.description: Count how many employees are listed using the COUNTA function.
problems.hint: Use =COUNTA( range ) to count non-empty cells
problems.explanation: COUNTA counts all non-empty cells. =COUNTA(A2:A5) = 4.

problems.title: Lowest Price
problems.description: Find the lowest product price using the MIN function.
problems.explanation: MIN returns the smallest number in a range. =MIN(B2:B6) = 6.5.

problems.title: Highest Revenue
problems.description: Find the highest monthly revenue using the MAX function.
problems.explanation: MAX returns the largest number. =MAX(B2:B6) = 61000.

problems.title: Second Highest Score
problems.description: Find the second highest score using the LARGE function.
problems.hint: Use =LARGE( range, k ) where k is the rank position
problems.explanation: LARGE returns the k-th largest value. =LARGE(B2:B7,2) returns 95 (second highest).

problems.title: Third Lowest Value
problems.description: Find the third smallest value using the SMALL function.
problems.explanation: SMALL returns the k-th smallest value. =SMALL(B2:B8,3) returns 39 (third lowest: 28, 32, 39).

problems.title: Sum Electronics Sales
problems.description: Sum the sales amount for only the Electronics category using SUMIF.
problems.explanation: =SUMIF(A2:A7,"Electronics",B2:B7) sums only amounts where category is Electronics: 150 + 220 = 370.

problems.title: Sum High Sales
problems.description: Sum all sales amounts greater than 300 using SUMIF.

problems.title: Sum Sales by Product and Region
problems.description: Sum sales for Product A in the East region using SUMIFS.
problems.hint: Use =SUMIFS( sum_range, criteria_range1, criteria1, criteria_range2, criteria2 )
problems.explanation: =SUMIFS(C2:C6,A2:A6,"Product A",B2:B6,"East") sums Sales where Product is A AND Region is East: 200 + 250 = 450.

problems.title: Sum Scores by Subject and Grade
problems.description: Sum scores for Math students who received an A grade using SUMIFS.
problems.hint: Use =SUMIFS( sum_range, criteria_range1, criteria1, criteria_range2, criteria2 )
problems.explanation: =SUMIFS(B2:B6,A2:A6,"Math",C2:C6,"A") sums scores where Subject=Math AND Grade=A: 85 + 95 = 180.

problems.title: Count Grade A
problems.description: Count how many students received an A grade using COUNTIF.
problems.explanation: =COUNTIF(B2:B8,"A") counts cells with value "A": 4 occurrences.

problems.title: Count High Sales
problems.description: Count how many sales were greater than 500 using COUNTIF.

problems.title: Count Math A Grades
problems.description: Count how many students got an A in Math using COUNTIFS.
problems.explanation: =COUNTIFS(A2:A6,"Math",C2:C6,"A") counts rows where Subject=Math AND Grade=A: 2 rows (Alice and Eve).

problems.title: Count East Region High Sales
problems.description: Count how many East region sales exceeded 200 using COUNTIFS.
problems.explanation: =COUNTIFS(B2:B6,"East",C2:C6,">200") counts East region sales >200: Widget C (300) and Widget D (250) = 2.

problems.title: Average Passing Score
problems.description: Calculate the average score for students who scored 70 or above using AVERAGEIF.
problems.explanation: =AVERAGEIF(B2:B7,">=70") averages scores >= 70: (85+92+78+95+88)/5 = 87.6. Score 62 is excluded.

problems.title: Pass or Fail
problems.description: Write an IF formula to determine if Alice passed. A score of 70 or above is a Pass, otherwise Fail.
problems.explanation: =IF(B2>=70,"Pass","Fail") checks if the score is >= 70. Since 85 >= 70, it returns "Pass".

problems.title: Bonus Eligibility
problems.description: Calculate the bonus: if sales are 10000 or more, the bonus is 500, otherwise 0.

problems.title: Discount Calculation
problems.description: Apply a 10% discount if the amount is greater than 100, otherwise no discount (0).
problems.explanation: =IF(B2>100,B2*0.1,0) checks if 150 > 100 (true), then returns 150*0.1 = 15.

problems.title: Scholarship Eligibility
problems.description: Determine if a student is eligible for a scholarship. They need a score of 85 or above AND attendance of 90% or above. Return 'Eligible' or 'Not Eligible'.
problems.explanation: =IF(AND(B2>=85,C2>=90),"Eligible","Not Eligible") checks both conditions. 88>=85 AND 95>=90 is TRUE, so returns "Eligible".

problems.title: Free Shipping
problems.description: Determine if an order gets free shipping. Shipping is free if the amount is greater than 50 OR the customer is a member. Return 'Free' or 'Paid'.
problems.explanation: =IF(OR(B2>50,C2="Yes"),"Free","Paid"). Amount 45 is not >50, but Member is "Yes", so OR is TRUE, returns "Free".

problems.title: Grade Letter
problems.description: Convert a numeric score to a letter grade: >=90 is A, >=80 is B, >=70 is C, below 70 is D.

problems.title: Commission Rate
problems.description: Calculate the commission rate: >=20000 is 15%, >=10000 is 10%, otherwise 5%.

problems.title: Find Employee Department
problems.description: Look up the department of employee ID 103 using VLOOKUP.
problems.explanation: =VLOOKUP(103,A2:C5,3,FALSE) looks up ID 103 in the table and returns the 3rd column (Department): "Engineering".

problems.title: Find Product Price
problems.description: Look up the price of Product C using VLOOKUP.
problems.explanation: =VLOOKUP("Product C",A2:B5,2,FALSE) finds Product C and returns the price from column 2: $15.00.

problems.title: Find Customer City
problems.description: Look up the city of customer ID 104 using VLOOKUP.
problems.explanation: =VLOOKUP(104,A2:B6,2,FALSE) finds ID 104 and returns "Houston" from column 2.

problems.title: Find Score by Name
problems.description: Use INDEX and MATCH to find Bob's score from the student list.
problems.explanation: =INDEX(B2:B5,MATCH("Bob",A2:A5,0)) matches "Bob" at position 3 (row 3), then INDEX returns the 3rd value from B2:B5 = 92.

problems.title: Find Price by Product ID
problems.description: Use INDEX and MATCH to find the price of product P003.
problems.explanation: =INDEX(C2:C5,MATCH("P003",A2:A5,0)) matches P003 at position 3, then INDEX returns 3rd price: 7.99.

problems.title: Two-Way Lookup: Sales by Person and Month
problems.description: Use INDEX and MATCH to find Bob's sales in March from the sales matrix.
problems.hint: Use =INDEX( data_range, MATCH( name, name_range, 0 ), MATCH( month, month_range, 0 ) )
problems.explanation: =INDEX(B2:D4,MATCH("Bob",A2:A4,0),MATCH("Mar",B1:D1,0)) finds Bob at row 2 and Mar at column 3, returning 90.

problems.title: Extract First Name
problems.description: Extract the first 4 characters from the full name using LEFT.
problems.explanation: =LEFT(A2,4) returns the first 4 characters of "John Smith": "John".

problems.title: Extract Last Name
problems.description: Extract the last 5 characters from the full name using RIGHT.
problems.explanation: =RIGHT(A2,5) returns the last 5 characters of "John Smith": "Smith".

problems.title: Extract Middle Characters
problems.description: Extract 3 characters starting from position 4 using MID.
problems.explanation: =MID(A2,4,3) starts at position 4 and returns 3 characters: "DEF".

problems.title: Count Characters
problems.description: Count how many characters are in the phrase 'Data Analytics' using LEN.
problems.explanation: =LEN(A2) counts all characters in "Data Analytics" including the space: 14.

problems.title: Clean Extra Spaces
problems.description: Remove leading and trailing spaces from the text using TRIM.
problems.explanation: =TRIM(A2) removes leading and trailing spaces from "  Hello World  ", returning "Hello World".

problems.title: Convert to Uppercase
problems.description: Convert the word 'hello' to uppercase using UPPER.
problems.explanation: =UPPER(A2) converts "hello" to uppercase: "HELLO".

problems.title: Convert to Title Case
problems.description: Convert the name 'john doe' to proper case using PROPER.
problems.hint: Use =PROPER( text ) to capitalize each word
problems.explanation: =PROPER(A2) capitalizes the first letter of each word: "john doe" becomes "John Doe".

problems.title: Combine First and Last Name
problems.description: Join the first name and last name with a space using CONCATENATE.
problems.explanation: =CONCATENATE(A2," ",B2) joins "John", a space, and "Doe" to give "John Doe".

problems.title: Join Items with Delimiter
problems.description: Join three fruit names separated by commas using CONCATENATE.
problems.explanation: =CONCATENATE(A2,", ",B2,", ",C2) joins the three fruit names with comma-space separators: "apple, banana, cherry".

problems.title: Find Remainder
problems.description: Find the remainder when 17 is divided by 5 using MOD.
problems.explanation: =MOD(17,5) divides 17 by 5 and returns the remainder. 17 / 5 = 3 remainder 2.

problems.title: Extract Integer Part
problems.description: Extract the integer part of 17.83 using INT.
problems.explanation: =INT(17.83) rounds down to the nearest integer: 17.

problems.title: Round Up to Nearest 0.5
problems.description: Round 4.27 up to the nearest multiple of 0.5 using CEILING.
problems.explanation: =CEILING(4.27,0.5) rounds 4.27 up to the nearest multiple of 0.5, which is 4.5.

problems.title: Round to Two Decimal Places
problems.description: Round 12.3456 to 2 decimal places using ROUND.
problems.explanation: =ROUND(12.3456,2) rounds to 2 decimal places = 12.35 (the third decimal 5 rounds up).

problems.title: Round Up to One Decimal
problems.description: Round 4.12 up to 1 decimal place using ROUNDUP.
problems.hint: Use =ROUNDUP( number, num_digits ) - always rounds away from zero
problems.explanation: =ROUNDUP(4.12,1) rounds 4.12 up to 1 decimal = 4.2 (always rounds up).

problems.title: Round Down to One Decimal
problems.description: Round 4.89 down to 1 decimal place using ROUNDDOWN.
problems.hint: Use =ROUNDDOWN( number, num_digits ) - always rounds toward zero
problems.explanation: =ROUNDDOWN(4.89,1) rounds 4.89 down to 1 decimal = 4.8 (always rounds down).

problems.title: Safe Division with Error Handling
problems.description: Divide 10 by 2, but if a division error occurs return 'Error' instead using IFERROR.
problems.explanation: =IFERROR(A2/B2,"Error") divides 10/2 = 5. No error occurs, so returns 5.

problems.title: Rank a Score
problems.description: Find the rank of a score of 92 among all scores using RANK (descending order, so 1 = highest).
problems.hint: Use =RANK( number, ref, order ). Omit order or use 0 for descending (default).
problems.explanation: =RANK(92,B2:B6) ranks 92 in descending order. The scores sorted: 95(1), 92(2), 88(3), 80(4), 75(5). So 92 ranks 2nd.

problems.title: Total Revenue with SUMPRODUCT
problems.description: Calculate total revenue by multiplying prices by quantities and summing the results using SUMPRODUCT.
problems.explanation: =SUMPRODUCT(B2:B4,C2:C4) multiplies each Price * Quantity and sums: (10*5)+(25*3)+(15*7) = 50+75+105 = 230.
